factorial_design.description: DynUNet-only mechanics debug: validates the 3-layer factorial pipeline with the simplest model that fits on RTX 2070 Super (8 GB VRAM). Isolates pipeline mechanics from model complexity (no SAM3, no VesselFM).

figures.description: R/ggplot2 publication figures (PDF + PNG + JSON sidecar)
figures.figure: Variance decomposition (condition vs fold vs residual)

tables.table: Cohen's d + Cliff's delta per pair

phase.name: DeepVess Data Preparation via Acquisition + Data Engineering Flows
phase.description:
DeepVess raw TIF files exist at data/raw/deepvess/ (26 TIF files, 1.9 GB, DVC-tracked via deepvess.dvc). DeepVess NIfTI: ZERO files — conversion has never been run. MiniVess: 70 NIfTI volumes already in Docker volume.
This phase uses the EXISTING Prefect flows in Docker: - Acquisition Flow (Flow 0): checks if DeepVess NIfTI exists → converts raw TIF → NIfTI using tifffile + nibabel → DVC versions the output - Data Engineering Flow (Flow 1): validates NIfTI metadata → runs Pandera schemas → discovers external dataset pairs → registers in splits
NO manual conversion scripts. NO standalone Python scripts. The Acquisition Flow's convert_formats_task() calls convert_dataset_formats() from src/minivess/data/format_conversion.py, which walks images/ and labels/ subdirectories and converts each .tif/.tiff to .nii.gz.
The DeepVess dataset (Haft-Javaherian et al. 2019) has: - 7 multiphoton volumes, mouse brain cortex, CC-BY-4.0 - Resolution: (1.0, 1.0, 1.7) μm — from external dataset registry
Pre-condition: Raw TIF files must be organized with images/ and labels/ subdirectories under data/raw/deepvess/ for convert_dataset_formats() to discover them. If the current layout is flat (26 TIFs at top level), Task 0.1 handles reorganization before running the Acquisition Flow.
phase.gate: DeepVess NIfTI files present at data/raw/deepvess/images/*.nii.gz and data/raw/deepvess/labels/*.nii.gz with at least 7 image-label pairs. Both MiniVess (70 vols) and DeepVess (7 vols) accessible inside Docker containers at /app/data/raw/minivess/ and /app/data/raw/deepvess/. Data Engineering Flow quality gates (Pandera, GE, DATA-CARE) all pass.

task.name: Run Acquisition Flow for DeepVess (TIF → NIfTI in Docker)
task.description:
Run the Acquisition Flow (Flow 0) via Docker Compose to convert DeepVess raw TIF files to NIfTI. The flow orchestrates:
1. check_dataset_status_task() — checks if NIfTI output already exists 2. download_dataset_task() — skips (raw TIFs already present via DVC) 3. convert_formats_task() — calls convert_dataset_formats() which: - Walks data/raw/deepvess/images/ and data/raw/deepvess/labels/ - Converts each .tif/.tiff to .nii.gz using tifffile + nibabel - Uses voxel_spacing from EXTERNAL_DATASETS["deepvess"].resolution_um 4. dvc_commit_datasets_task() — DVC-versions the converted NIfTI output 5. log_acquisition_provenance_task() — logs to MLflow
PRE-REQUISITE: Verify that data/raw/deepvess/ has images/ and labels/ subdirectories (required by convert_dataset_formats). If the 26 TIF files are in a flat layout, reorganize first: - GroundTruthImage*.tif → data/raw/deepvess/images/ - GroundTruthLabel*.tif → data/raw/deepvess/labels/ - Unlabeled inference volumes: excluded (no labels for TEST evaluation)
The Acquisition Flow uses AcquisitionConfig with: datasets=["deepvess"], skip_existing=True, convert_formats=True
Docker execution (STOP Protocol — Rule 19): S: Running in Docker via docker-compose.flows.yml::acquisition T: Prefect orchestration active (Flow 0 decorator) O: raw_data:/app/data/raw volume-mounted (rw) P: Reproducible — Docker image + DVC-tracked input + config
task.command:
# Populate Docker volume with raw DeepVess TIFs docker run --rm --user root \ -v deployment_raw_data:/app/data/raw \ -v $(pwd)/data:/src:ro \ ubuntu:22.04 \ sh -c "cp -r /src/raw/deepvess /app/data/raw/ && chown -R 1000:1000 /app/data/raw/deepvess"
# Run Acquisition Flow for DeepVess only docker compose --env-file .env -f deployment/docker-compose.flows.yml run --rm \ -e ACQUISITION_DATASETS=deepvess \ acquisition
task.acceptance: Acquisition Flow completes with DatasetAcquisitionStatus.READY or DOWNLOADED for deepvess. NIfTI files exist at data/raw/deepvess/images/*.nii.gz and data/raw/deepvess/labels/*.nii.gz. At least 7 image-label pairs converted. MLflow logs acquisition provenance (acq_n_datasets, acq_total_volumes). DVC tracking updated for the converted NIfTI files.

task.name: Run Data Engineering Flow for DeepVess (validate + register)
task.description:
Run the Data Engineering Flow (Flow 1) via Docker Compose with DeepVess as an external dataset. The flow orchestrates:
1. discover_data_task() — discovers MiniVess primary pairs 2. validate_data_task() — basic key-presence validation 3. _run_quality_gates() — advanced validation chain: - extract_nifti_metadata_task() — reads NIfTI headers (shape, spacing, affine) - pandera_gate_task() — NiftiMetadataSchema validation - ge_gate_task() — Great Expectations nifti_metadata_suite - datacare_gate_task() — DATA-CARE quality assessment - deepchecks_gate_task() — DeepChecks data integrity 4. split_data_task() — generates K-fold splits (MiniVess only) 5. validate_external_data_task("deepvess") — discovers DeepVess pairs 6. log_data_provenance_task() — logs to MLflow
The external_dirs parameter passes DeepVess path: external_dirs={"deepvess": Path("/app/data/raw/deepvess")}
Docker execution (STOP Protocol): S: Running in Docker via docker-compose.flows.yml::data T: Prefect orchestration active (Flow 1 decorator) O: raw_data:/app/data/raw:ro, configs_splits:/app/configs/splits (rw) P: Reproducible — Docker image + validated input + config
task.command: docker compose --env-file .env -f deployment/docker-compose.flows.yml run --rm \ -e EXTERNAL_DATASETS=deepvess \ data
task.acceptance: Data Engineering Flow completes successfully. All quality gates pass (Pandera, GE, DATA-CARE, DeepChecks) for DeepVess NIfTI. validate_external_data_task("deepvess") discovers 7 pairs. NIfTI metadata extracted: shapes, voxel spacing (1.0, 1.0, 1.7), valid affines. MLflow logs data provenance (data_n_volumes, data_hash).

task.name: Populate Docker data volume with DeepVess NIfTI
task.description:
Ensure the Docker named volume (deployment_raw_data) contains both MiniVess and DeepVess NIfTI data. The Acquisition Flow (Task 0.1) writes NIfTI output to the raw_data volume. Verify the volume state.
The raw_data volume is mounted at /app/data/raw in flow containers: - /app/data/raw/minivess/imagesTr/ (70 volumes — already populated) - /app/data/raw/deepvess/images/ (7 volumes — from Task 0.1) - /app/data/raw/deepvess/labels/ (7 volumes — from Task 0.1)
If MiniVess is not yet in the volume (first-time setup), populate it: docker run --rm --user root \ -v deployment_raw_data:/app/data/raw \ -v $(pwd)/data:/src:ro \ ubuntu:22.04 \ sh -c "cp -r /src/raw/minivess /app/data/raw/ && chown -R 1000:1000 /app/data/raw"
task.command: # Verify volume contents docker run --rm -v deployment_raw_data:/app/data/raw:ro ubuntu:22.04 \ sh -c "echo '=== MiniVess ===' && ls /app/data/raw/minivess/imagesTr/ | wc -l && \ echo '=== DeepVess images ===' && ls /app/data/raw/deepvess/images/ | wc -l && \ echo '=== DeepVess labels ===' && ls /app/data/raw/deepvess/labels/ | wc -l"
task.acceptance: MiniVess: 70 NIfTI volumes at /app/data/raw/minivess/imagesTr/ DeepVess: 7 NIfTI images at /app/data/raw/deepvess/images/ DeepVess: 7 NIfTI labels at /app/data/raw/deepvess/labels/ All files owned by UID 1000 (minivess user inside containers).

task.name: Verify both MiniVess + DeepVess accessible in Docker
task.description:
End-to-end verification: run a lightweight check inside the actual flow containers (not ubuntu:22.04) to confirm that both datasets are readable by the Python code that will consume them during training and analysis.
This verifies: 1. Volume mounts are correct in docker-compose.flows.yml 2. File permissions allow the minivess user (UID 1000) to read 3. NIfTI files are loadable by nibabel (not corrupt) 4. discover_external_test_pairs("deepvess") returns 7 pairs 5. discover_nifti_pairs() for MiniVess returns 70 pairs
Uses the data container (same base image as train/analyze) to run a Python verification script inside Docker.
task.command:
docker compose --env-file .env -f deployment/docker-compose.flows.yml run --rm \ data python -c " import nibabel as nib from pathlib import Path from minivess.data.external_datasets import discover_external_test_pairs from minivess.data.loader import discover_nifti_pairs
# MiniVess mv_pairs = discover_nifti_pairs(Path('/app/data/raw/minivess')) print(f'MiniVess: {len(mv_pairs)} pairs') assert len(mv_pairs) == 70, f'Expected 70 MiniVess pairs, got {len(mv_pairs)}'
# DeepVess dv_pairs = discover_external_test_pairs(Path('/app/data/raw/deepvess'), 'deepvess') print(f'DeepVess: {len(dv_pairs)} pairs') assert len(dv_pairs) == 7, f'Expected 7 DeepVess pairs, got {len(dv_pairs)}'
# Spot-check: load one NIfTI from each dataset mv_img = nib.load(mv_pairs[0]['image']) print(f'MiniVess sample shape: {mv_img.shape}') dv_img = nib.load(dv_pairs[0]['image']) print(f'DeepVess sample shape: {dv_img.shape}')
print('PHASE 0 GATE: PASSED — both datasets accessible in Docker') "
task.acceptance: MiniVess: 70 pairs discovered by discover_nifti_pairs() DeepVess: 7 pairs discovered by discover_external_test_pairs() At least one NIfTI from each dataset loads without error via nibabel. Print: "PHASE 0 GATE: PASSED — both datasets accessible in Docker"

phase.name: Configuration
phase.description: Create all YAML configuration files following established patterns from configs/factorial/smoke_local.yaml and configs/factorial/debug.yaml. Every parameter comes from config — zero hardcoded values (Rule 29).
phase.gate: All YAML files parse cleanly. make test-staging passes with new configs.

task.name: Create 2-fold half-data splits file
task.description:
Generate configs/splits/debug_half_2fold.json following the pattern of configs/splits/debug_half_1fold.json but with 2 folds.
Use seed=42 (from config, not hardcoded in code). ~35 train volumes, ~12 val volumes per fold. Folds are NON-overlapping validation sets (standard k-fold CV).
Implementation: use existing split generation code, or manually construct from the 3fold_seed42.json by combining folds 0+1 for fold-0 val and folds 1+2 for fold-1 val. Ensure train/val partition is deterministic.
task.acceptance: JSON parses cleanly. 2 folds, each with ~35 train and ~12 val entries. No train/val overlap within a fold. All file paths reference data/raw/minivess/imagesTr/ and labelsTr/.

task.name: Create factorial config YAML
task.description:
Create configs/factorial/local_dynunet_mechanics.yaml following the established pattern. This is the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for the experiment.
Key differences from debug.yaml: - model_family: [dynunet] only (not 4 models) - loss_name: [dice_ce, cbdice_cldice] (2 losses) - aux_calibration: [false, true] - post_training method: [none, swag] - recalibration: [none] (no temperature_scaling) - ensemble_strategy: [per_loss_single_best, all_loss_single_best] - max_epochs: 15 - num_folds: 2 - infrastructure.cloud_config: local - No zero_shot_baselines (DynUNet-only mechanics test) - splits_file: configs/splits/debug_half_2fold.json - MLflow: DagsHub (from .env MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI)
task.yaml_skeleton:
experiment_name: local_dynunet_mechanics description: "Local DynUNet mechanics debug: 16 conditions, 2 folds, 15 epochs" debug: true
factors: training: model_family: - dynunet loss_name: - dice_ce - cbdice_cldice aux_calibration: - false - true
post_training: method: - none - swag recalibration: - none
analysis: ensemble_strategy: - per_loss_single_best - all_loss_single_best
zero_shot_baselines: []
fixed: max_epochs: 15 num_folds: 2 max_train_volumes: 35 max_val_volumes: 12 compute: auto learning_rate: 1.0e-3 batch_size: 2
splits_file: configs/splits/debug_half_2fold.json
mlflow: training_experiment: local_dynunet_mechanics_training evaluation_experiment: local_dynunet_mechanics_evaluation biostatistics_experiment: local_dynunet_mechanics_biostatistics
biostatistics: anova_strategy: layered alpha: 0.05 co_primary_metrics: - cldice - masd foil_metrics: - dsc
post_training: methods: - none - swag
infrastructure: cloud_config: local
flows: - train - analyze - biostatistics
task.acceptance: YAML parses cleanly with yaml.safe_load(). 4 Layer A cells x 2 Layer B x 2 Layer C = 16 total conditions. infrastructure.cloud_config is "local". No cloud-specific keys (no skypilot_yaml, no region_config).

task.name: Create per-condition experiment configs
task.description:
Create 4 Hydra experiment configs (one per training cell): - local_dynunet_dice_ce.yaml (loss=dice_ce, aux_calib=false) - local_dynunet_dice_ce_calib.yaml (loss=dice_ce, aux_calib=true) - local_dynunet_cbdice_cldice.yaml (loss=cbdice_cldice, aux_calib=false) - local_dynunet_cbdice_cldice_calib.yaml (loss=cbdice_cldice, aux_calib=true)
Each config overrides base defaults following the pattern in configs/experiment/mini_experiment_dice_ce.yaml.
IMPORTANT: These configs set ONLY the varying factors. Fixed parameters (max_epochs, batch_size, num_folds) come from the factorial YAML. The experiment configs are Hydra composition overrides, not standalone.
task.acceptance: Each YAML parses cleanly. Each sets model, loss, and aux_calib fields. experiment_name matches factorial YAML training_experiment.

task.name: Create biostatistics config
task.description:
Create configs/biostatistics/local_dynunet.yaml following the pattern of configs/biostatistics/smoke_local.yaml.
Key settings: - experiment_names: [local_dynunet_mechanics_evaluation] - factorial_yaml: configs/factorial/local_dynunet_mechanics.yaml - metrics: [dsc, hd95, assd, nsd, cldice, be_0, be_1, junction_f1, cal_ece, cal_mce, cal_brier, cal_nll, cal_ace, cal_ba_ece] - primary_metric: cldice - alpha: 0.05 (from BiostatisticsConfig, not hardcoded) - n_bootstrap: 2000 (reduced from 10000 for local speed) - seed: 42 - ROPE values per reviewer consensus - min_folds_per_condition: 2 (matching num_folds=2) - min_conditions: 4 (matching 4 training cells) - output_dir: outputs/biostatistics_local_dynunet
task.acceptance: YAML parses cleanly. BiostatisticsConfig(**yaml.safe_load(path)) validates without errors. factorial_yaml points to the correct factorial config. All 14 metrics listed (8 segmentation + 6 calibration).

phase.name: Docker Image Rebuild
phase.description:
Rebuild Docker base images to include current source code and any new dependencies. The biostatistics flow needs R/ggplot2 — this requires a multi-stage addition to Dockerfile.base-cpu or Dockerfile.biostatistics.
CURRENT STATE: Dockerfile.biostatistics uses minivess-base-cpu:latest (Tier B). The base-cpu image is Python-only (scipy/pandas/DuckDB). R is NOT installed. The current biostatistics_figures.py uses matplotlib/seaborn (Python).
PLAN: Add R/ggplot2 to the biostatistics Docker image via a multi-stage build that installs R + ggplot2 in the builder stage and copies the R installation to the runner. Create R scripts that read the JSON exports from biostatistics_r_export.py.
phase.gate: All 3 Docker images build successfully. Healthchecks pass.

task.name: Rebuild GPU base image
task.description: Rebuild minivess-base:latest with current src/ and configs/. This is the foundation for train and analyze flows.
task.command: DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1 docker build \ --build-arg UID=$(id -u) --build-arg GID=$(id -g) \ --build-arg GIT_COMMIT=$(git rev-parse HEAD) \ --build-arg BUILD_DATE=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") \ -t minivess-base:latest \ -f deployment/docker/Dockerfile.base .
task.acceptance: docker images shows minivess-base:latest with recent timestamp. python -c "import minivess" works inside container.

task.name: Rebuild CPU base image
task.description: Rebuild minivess-base-cpu:latest with current source. Foundation for biostatistics flow.
task.command: DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1 docker build \ --build-arg UID=$(id -u) --build-arg GID=$(id -g) \ --build-arg GIT_COMMIT=$(git rev-parse HEAD) \ --build-arg BUILD_DATE=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") \ -t minivess-base-cpu:latest \ -f deployment/docker/Dockerfile.base-cpu .
task.acceptance: docker images shows minivess-base-cpu:latest with recent timestamp.

task.name: Add R/ggplot2 to biostatistics Docker image
task.description:
Modify Dockerfile.biostatistics to install R + ggplot2 + required R packages. Two approaches (decide during implementation):
APPROACH A (preferred): Multi-stage addition to Dockerfile.biostatistics. Add R installation in the FROM stage, keeping the thin Dockerfile pattern. Install R packages: ggplot2, dplyr, tidyr, jsonlite, scales, patchwork, ggrepel, viridis, Cairo (for PDF/PNG output).
APPROACH B (if A bloats image): Separate R container that reads JSON from a shared volume. Less integrated but smaller images.
The key insight: Python biostatistics flow exports JSON/CSV via biostatistics_r_export.py (already implemented). R scripts read these files and produce PDF/PNG figures. No rpy2 needed.
R package install is slow (~15 min). Use BuildKit cache mount for the R library directory to avoid re-downloading on rebuilds.
task.dockerfile_sketch:
# Dockerfile.biostatistics — with R/ggplot2 multi-stage FROM minivess-base-cpu:latest AS python-base
# ── R installation stage ────────────────────────────────────────────── FROM rocker/r-ver:4.4.0 AS r-builder
# Install R packages with cache mount RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/usr/local/lib/R/site-library-cache \ install2.r --error --skipinstalled \ ggplot2 dplyr tidyr jsonlite scales patchwork \ ggrepel viridis Cairo forcats ggbeeswarm scmamp
# ── Final stage ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────── FROM python-base
# Copy R installation from r-builder COPY --from=r-builder /usr/local/lib/R /usr/local/lib/R COPY --from=r-builder /usr/local/bin/R /usr/local/bin/R COPY --from=r-builder /usr/local/bin/Rscript /usr/local/bin/Rscript # R shared libraries COPY --from=r-builder /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libR* /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/
# Copy R figure scripts COPY --chown=minivess:minivess deployment/r_scripts/ /app/r_scripts/
LABEL flow="biostatistics" LABEL description="Biostatistics flow — stats + R/ggplot2 figures + LaTeX tables" LABEL tier="cpu"
# ... (rest as existing Dockerfile.biostatistics)
task.acceptance: docker build -t minivess-biostatistics:latest succeeds. Rscript -e "library(ggplot2); cat('OK\n')" works inside container. python -c "import minivess" works inside container. Image size is reasonable (< 3 GB).
task.note: CRITICAL: This is the most complex task in the plan. The rocker/r-ver base image is Debian-based, same as python:3.13-slim-bookworm, so library copying should work. If it does not, fall back to installing R directly in the runner stage with apt-get (increases image size but guarantees compatibility). Test with a minimal R script first before installing all packages.

task.name: Build flow images
task.description: Build the thin flow images on top of the refreshed base images.
task.command: docker compose -f deployment/docker-compose.flows.yml build train analyze biostatistics
task.acceptance: All 3 flow images built successfully. docker images shows minivess-train:latest, minivess-analyze:latest, minivess-biostatistics:latest with recent timestamps.

task.name: Validate Docker network and infrastructure stack
task.description: Ensure the Docker infrastructure stack is running (MLflow, Prefect, MinIO). For this local experiment, MLflow tracking uses DagsHub (MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI from .env), so the local MLflow container is optional. Prefect server is required for flow orchestration.
task.command: # Create network if not exists docker network create minivess-network 2>/dev/null || true # Start Prefect (required for flow orchestration) docker compose -f deployment/docker-compose.yml --profile dev up -d prefect # Verify Prefect API is reachable curl -sf http://localhost:4200/api/health || echo "Prefect not ready"
task.acceptance: docker network ls shows minivess-network. Prefect health endpoint returns 200. .env has MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI set to DagsHub URL.

phase.name: Training
phase.description:
Execute 8 training runs (4 conditions x 2 folds) sequentially on local GPU. Each run trains DynUNet for 15 epochs on half MiniVess data.
EXECUTION MODEL: - Docker Compose run with GPU access (CDI: nvidia.com/gpu=all) - Prefect flow orchestration (train_flow.py) - MLflow tracking to DagsHub - Post-training SWAG runs as subflow inside the same container - Observability: GPU heartbeat, structured JSONL logging, CUDA guard
SEQUENTIAL because: local single GPU, can only run 1 training at a time. The launcher script handles the loop.
Post-training (Layer B) runs INSIDE the training container as a subflow. "none" is free (just the trained checkpoint). "swag" runs SWAG collection (Maddox et al. 2019) after training completes.
phase.gate: All 8 training runs SUCCEEDED in MLflow. Checkpoints logged as MLflow artifacts. Each run has: val/cldice, val/dsc metrics, checkpoint artifact, config artifact.

task.name: Create launcher script
task.description:
Create scripts/run_local_dynunet_mechanics.sh — the SINGLE COMMAND that reproduces the entire experiment end-to-end.
The script: 1. Validates prerequisites (.env, Docker images, data, network) 2. Loops over 4 conditions x 2 folds = 8 training runs 3. Each run: docker compose run --rm --shm-size 8g train (with env vars) 4. After all training: runs analysis flow (Phase 4) 5. After analysis: runs biostatistics flow (Phase 5) 6. Reports final summary
CRITICAL: Uses Docker Compose (not SkyPilot) since this is local. Passes EXPERIMENT, HYDRA_OVERRIDES, FOLD_ID etc. as -e env vars. Uses --env-file .env for DagsHub credentials (Rule: Docker Compose V2 needs --env-file explicitly).
task.script_skeleton:
#!/usr/bin/env bash # run_local_dynunet_mechanics.sh — Reproducible 3-flow local pipeline test # # Single command to run the full DynUNet mechanics debug: # Train (8 runs) → Analyze (ensemble + DeepVess) → Biostatistics (R figures) # # Usage: # ./scripts/run_local_dynunet_mechanics.sh # ./scripts/run_local_dynunet_mechanics.sh --dry-run # ./scripts/run_local_dynunet_mechanics.sh --skip-training # analysis + biostats only # # Prerequisites: # - .env with MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI, DAGSHUB_TOKEN, HF_TOKEN # - Docker images built (make build-bases && docker compose -f ... build) # - MiniVess data at data/raw/minivess/ # - DeepVess NIfTI at data/raw/deepvess/nifti/ # - Docker network: docker network create minivess-network # # Time estimate: ~4 hours total (3.3h training + 0.3h analysis + 0.2h biostats)
set -euo pipefail
REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)" CONFIG_FILE="configs/factorial/local_dynunet_mechanics.yaml" ENV_FILE="${REPO_ROOT}/.env" COMPOSE_FILE="${REPO_ROOT}/deployment/docker-compose.flows.yml" TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ) JOB_LOG="${REPO_ROOT}/outputs/${TIMESTAMP}_local_dynunet_mechanics.log"
# ... (flags, validation, preflight checks)
# ─── Phase 3: Training ────────────────────────────────────────────── MODELS=("dynunet") LOSSES=("dice_ce" "cbdice_cldice") CALIBS=("false" "true") FOLDS=(0 1) PT_METHODS="none,swag"
CONDITION=0 for loss in "${LOSSES[@]}"; do for calib in "${CALIBS[@]}"; do for fold in "${FOLDS[@]}"; do CONDITION=$((CONDITION + 1)) echo "[${CONDITION}/8] dynunet x ${loss} x calib=${calib} x fold=${fold}" docker compose --env-file "${ENV_FILE}" -f "${COMPOSE_FILE}" run --rm \ --shm-size 8g \ -e MODEL_FAMILY=dynunet \ -e LOSS_NAME="${loss}" \ -e FOLD_ID="${fold}" \ -e WITH_AUX_CALIB="${calib}" \ -e MAX_EPOCHS=15 \ -e MAX_TRAIN_VOLUMES=35 \ -e MAX_VAL_VOLUMES=12 \ -e EXPERIMENT_NAME=local_dynunet_mechanics_training \ -e POST_TRAINING_METHODS="${PT_METHODS}" \ -e BATCH_SIZE=2 \ train done done done
# ─── Phase 4: Analysis ────────────────────────────────────────────── docker compose --env-file "${ENV_FILE}" -f "${COMPOSE_FILE}" run --rm \ -e FACTORIAL_YAML="${CONFIG_FILE}" \ -e EVAL_DATASETS="minivess,deepvess" \ analyze
# ─── Phase 5: Biostatistics ───────────────────────────────────────── docker compose --env-file "${ENV_FILE}" -f "${COMPOSE_FILE}" run --rm \ -e BIOSTATISTICS_CONFIG=configs/biostatistics/local_dynunet.yaml \ biostatistics
task.acceptance: Script is executable (chmod +x). --dry-run prints all 8 docker compose run commands without executing. Passes shellcheck with no errors. Uses --env-file .env (Docker Compose V2 requirement). Uses --shm-size 8g for GPU flows (SIGBUS prevention).

task.name: Execute training runs
task.description:
Run the 8 training jobs sequentially via the launcher script. Monitor via: - docker logs minivess-flow-train (real-time) - DagsHub MLflow UI (experiment: local_dynunet_mechanics_training) - outputs/logs/ (structured JSONL events)
Expected per run: - ~25 min wall clock - ~4-5 GB VRAM usage (DynUNet + BS=2 + half data) - val/cldice, val/dsc, val/loss logged per epoch - Best checkpoint saved + logged to MLflow
SWAG subflow adds ~5 min per run (2nd moment collection, SWAG checkpoint).
Total: 8 runs x 30 min (with SWAG) = ~4 hours.

monitoring.check: MLflow UI shows new run with correct tags
SWAG checkpoint logged (model/swag.pt) for SWAG runs
No OOM (DynUNet BS=2 fits in 8 GB)
No NaN in loss (DynUNet uses FP32, no BF16 issues)

task.failure_protocol: If a run fails: 1. Check docker logs for error (OOM? NaN? MLflow auth?) 2. Check MLflow for partial metrics 3. If OOM: reduce batch_size to 1 + gradient_accumulation_steps=2 4. If MLflow auth: verify DAGSHUB_TOKEN in .env 5. If NaN: check loss function compatibility with data 6. Re-run ONLY the failed condition (script is idempotent with --resume)
task.acceptance: 8/8 training runs show SUCCEEDED in MLflow. Each run has: - val/cldice > 0.3 (sanity check — 15 epochs is not much) - val/dsc > 0.5 (sanity check) - checkpoint/best.pt artifact - config/resolved_config.yaml artifact - train/loss monotonically decreasing (broadly) SWAG runs additionally have: - model/swag.pt artifact

phase.name: Analysis
phase.description:
Run the Analysis Flow (Flow 3) to: 1. Discover training runs from MLflow (8 runs) 2. Build ensemble models using 2 strategies: - per_loss_single_best: best model per loss function - all_loss_single_best: best model across all losses 3. Evaluate all models (single + ensemble) on: - MiniVess validation set (per-volume metrics) - DeepVess external test set (per-volume metrics, OBLIGATORY) 4. Log evaluation results to MLflow (experiment: local_dynunet_mechanics_evaluation) 5. Compute per-volume metrics for paired bootstrap in biostatistics
EXECUTION: Docker container with GPU (sliding_window_inference needs GPU). Reads checkpoints from MLflow artifacts. Writes evaluation results to MLflow.
DeepVess evaluation is OBLIGATORY (CLAUDE.md). This is the ONLY external test dataset. Results tagged with test/deepvess/{metric} prefix.
phase.gate: 32 evaluation configs completed in MLflow. Per-volume metrics logged for both MiniVess val and DeepVess test. At least N=12 val volumes and N=7 DeepVess volumes per evaluation.

task.name: Execute analysis flow
task.description:
Run analysis flow via Docker Compose: docker compose --env-file .env -f deployment/docker-compose.flows.yml run --rm \ --shm-size 8g \ -e FACTORIAL_YAML=configs/factorial/local_dynunet_mechanics.yaml \ -e EVAL_DATASETS=minivess,deepvess \ analyze
The analysis flow: 1. Queries MLflow for all runs in local_dynunet_mechanics_training 2. Groups runs by condition (model x loss x aux_calib) 3. For each condition x fold: loads checkpoint, runs sliding_window_inference 4. Evaluates on val set + DeepVess test set 5. Builds ensemble models (2 strategies) 6. Evaluates ensembles on both datasets 7. Logs all per-volume metrics to MLflow 8. Tags champion model per metric
Per-volume metrics are critical for downstream biostatistics: - N=12 val volumes per fold allows paired bootstrap - N=7 DeepVess volumes allows external test statistics - Metrics: dsc, hd95, assd, nsd, cldice, be_0, be_1, junction_f1 - Calibration metrics: cal_ece, cal_mce, cal_brier, cal_nll, cal_ace, cal_ba_ece
task.acceptance: MLflow experiment local_dynunet_mechanics_evaluation has runs. Each evaluation run has per-volume metrics logged. DeepVess test metrics present with test/deepvess/ prefix. MiniVess val metrics present with eval/minivess/ prefix. Ensemble evaluation runs present for both strategies.

task.name: Validate per-volume metric completeness
task.description:
Query MLflow to verify that per-volume metrics were logged correctly. Each evaluation should have: - Per-volume DSC, clDice for MiniVess val (N=12 per fold) - Per-volume DSC, clDice for DeepVess test (N=7) - Summary statistics (mean, std, CI)
This data feeds directly into the biostatistics paired bootstrap. If per-volume metrics are missing, the biostatistics flow will fail.
task.command: python3 -c " import mlflow mlflow.set_tracking_uri('$MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI') exp = mlflow.get_experiment_by_name('local_dynunet_mechanics_evaluation') runs = mlflow.search_runs(experiment_ids=[exp.experiment_id]) print(f'Evaluation runs: {len(runs)}') for _, run in runs.iterrows(): metrics = [k for k in run.index if k.startswith('metrics.')] print(f' {run[\"run_id\"][:8]}: {len(metrics)} metrics') "
task.acceptance: At least 32 evaluation runs found. Each run has per-volume metrics. DeepVess metrics present for at least 7 volumes.

phase.name: Biostatistics
phase.description:
Run the Biostatistics Flow (Flow 5b) to: 1. Discover evaluation runs from MLflow 2. Materialize per-volume metrics into DuckDB 3. Compute statistical analyses: - Pairwise comparisons (stratified permutation test) - Effect sizes (Cohen's d, Cliff's delta, VDA) - Bayesian signed-rank tests (Benavoli et al. 2017) - Factorial ANOVA (3 factors: loss x calibration x post-training) - Variance decomposition (condition vs fold vs residual) - Friedman test + Nemenyi post-hoc (rankings) - Specification curve analysis - Rank concordance (bootstrap stability) 4. Export JSON/CSV for R consumption (biostatistics_r_export.py) 5. Generate R/ggplot2 figures (render_all_figures.R) 6. Generate LaTeX tables 7. Generate CONSORT data + TRIPOD mapping 8. Log all artifacts to MLflow
EXECUTION: CPU Docker container (no GPU needed for stats + R). Reads from MLflow evaluation experiment. Writes to outputs/biostatistics_local_dynunet/.
phase.gate: All figures (PDF + PNG + JSON sidecar) and tables (LaTeX + JSON sidecar) generated. DuckDB materialized. CONSORT JSON present. All artifacts logged to MLflow biostatistics experiment.

task.name: Execute biostatistics flow
task.description:
Run biostatistics flow via Docker Compose: docker compose --env-file .env -f deployment/docker-compose.flows.yml run --rm \ -e BIOSTATISTICS_CONFIG=configs/biostatistics/local_dynunet.yaml \ biostatistics
The flow pipeline: 1. task_discover_source_runs: finds evaluation runs in MLflow 2. task_validate_source_completeness: checks min_folds, min_conditions 3. task_build_duckdb: materializes per-volume metrics into DuckDB 4. task_compute_pairwise: stratified permutation + bootstrap CIs 5. task_compute_bayesian: Bayesian signed-rank (ROPE) 6. task_compute_anova: 3-factor factorial ANOVA 7. task_compute_variance: ICC + Friedman 8. task_compute_rankings: mean ranks + Nemenyi CD 9. task_compute_specification_curve: multiverse analysis 10. task_export_r_data: JSON/CSV for R 11. task_render_r_figures: Rscript render_all_figures.R 12. task_generate_tables: LaTeX with booktabs 13. task_generate_consort: CONSORT flow diagram data 14. task_log_to_mlflow: upload all artifacts
task.acceptance: Prefect flow completes without errors. Output directory has: figures/, tables/, r_data/, duckdb/ All JSON sidecars present alongside figures and tables.

task.name: Validate R figures
task.description:
Check that R/ggplot2 produced all expected figures: - F3_effect_size_heatmap.pdf + .png + .json - F4_forest_plot.pdf + .png + .json - F6_specification_curve.pdf + .png + .json - F7_generalization_gap.pdf + .png + .json - F8_cd_diagram.pdf + .png + .json - F2_consort_flow.pdf + .png + .json - F5_interaction_plot.pdf + .png + .json - F9_variance_lollipop.pdf + .png + .json
Each figure should be publication-quality: - 300 DPI minimum - Okabe-Ito colorblind-safe palette - 9pt font size - PDF fonttype 42 (TrueType, not bitmap) - Proper axis labels, legend, title
task.acceptance: All expected figure files exist (PDF + PNG). Each has a JSON sidecar with metadata (figure_id, generation_timestamp, data_source, n_datapoints). PDFs are non-empty and openable. PNGs are at least 300 DPI (check with: identify -verbose figure.png | grep Resolution).

task.name: Validate LaTeX tables
task.description:
Check that all expected tables were generated: - T1_comparison.tex + .json - T2_effect_sizes.tex + .json - T3_variance_decomposition.tex + .json - T4_rankings.tex + .json - T5_anova.tex + .json - T6_power_analysis.tex + .json
Each table should: - Use booktabs formatting (\toprule, \midrule, \bottomrule) - Have proper column alignment - Include caption and label - Compile with pdflatex without errors
task.acceptance: All expected .tex files exist. Each has a JSON sidecar. Tables contain actual data (not placeholders). booktabs macros present in .tex files.

task.name: Validate statistical correctness
task.description: Sanity checks on the statistical results: 1. All p-values in [0, 1] 2. Bonferroni/Holm-corrected p-values >= uncorrected p-values 3. Cohen's d magnitude reasonable for 15-epoch models 4. Cliff's delta in [-1, 1] 5. ROPE probabilities sum to ~1 (left + rope + right) 6. ICC values in [0, 1] 7. Variance decomposition percentages sum to 100% 8. Rankings are valid permutations of condition names 9. DeepVess metrics present in generalization gap figure data 10. Bootstrap CIs are narrower than the metric range
task.acceptance: All sanity checks pass. No NaN or Inf values in any statistical output. CONSORT JSON has correct counts (matching factorial design).

phase.name: Verification
phase.description: Final verification that the full pipeline produced correct, complete, and reproducible results. This phase does NOT generate new artifacts — it validates what Phases 3-5 produced.
phase.gate: All verification criteria pass. Experiment is reproducible from a single command.

task.name: Artifact inventory
task.description:
Verify ALL expected artifacts exist:
MLflow (DagsHub): - local_dynunet_mechanics_training: 8 runs, each with checkpoint + config - local_dynunet_mechanics_evaluation: 32 evaluation configs - local_dynunet_mechanics_biostatistics: 1 run with all figures/tables
Local filesystem: - outputs/biostatistics_local_dynunet/figures/ (9 figures x 3 formats) - outputs/biostatistics_local_dynunet/tables/ (6 tables x 2 formats) - outputs/biostatistics_local_dynunet/r_data/ (4 JSON files) - outputs/biostatistics_local_dynunet/duckdb/ (materialized database) - outputs/biostatistics_local_dynunet/consort_data.json - outputs/biostatistics_local_dynunet/tripod_mapping.json
task.acceptance: All artifacts present. No placeholder or empty files. MLflow experiment names match factorial YAML.

task.name: Reproducibility check
task.description:
Verify the experiment can be reproduced: 1. All config files are checked into git 2. splits file is deterministic (seed=42) 3. Docker images have git commit label 4. .env.example documents all required variables 5. scripts/run_local_dynunet_mechanics.sh is self-contained
The goal: another researcher clones the repo, copies .env.example to .env, fills in credentials, builds Docker images, and runs the script. They should get statistically equivalent results.
task.acceptance: git status shows configs tracked (not in .gitignore). Docker image labels contain correct git commit hash. .env.example lists all variables used by the launcher script. Script does not depend on any unversioned state.

task.name: DeepVess generalization assessment
task.description:
Key scientific finding from this experiment: - How much does DynUNet performance degrade from MiniVess val to DeepVess test? - Does loss function choice affect generalization gap? - Does calibration affect generalization?
This is the PLATFORM FRAMING contribution (CLAUDE.md): the platform's ability to automatically assess generalization across datasets with subgroup analysis. The actual numbers are standard practice.
Extract from the generalization gap figure (F7) data: - Mean DSC drop from MiniVess to DeepVess - Mean clDice drop - Per-condition gap magnitude
task.acceptance: Generalization gap data present in F7 JSON sidecar. Both MiniVess and DeepVess metrics present per condition. Gap magnitude is plausible (positive drop expected for cross-dataset eval).

task.name: Test suite validation
task.description: Run make test-staging to verify that the new configs and scripts don't break any existing tests.
task.acceptance: 0 failures, 0 errors. Skip count documented and justified. New configs are discoverable by existing test infrastructure.

observability.description: All observability is wired through existing infrastructure. No new observability code needed — just ensure it is activated.

component.name: CUDA Guard
component.description: gpu_flow_observability_context() in train_flow.py and analysis_flow.py checks CUDA availability before starting. Fails loud if no GPU.

component.name: GPU Heartbeat
component.description: Background thread logging GPU utilization, temperature, VRAM every 30s. Configured via GPU_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_S in docker-compose.flows.yml. Alerts if utilization <5% for >120s (stall detection).

component.name: Structured JSONL Logging
component.description: StructuredEventLogger writes flow_start, epoch_end, flow_end events to outputs/logs/events.jsonl. Machine-parseable for post-hoc analysis.

component.name: Docker HEALTHCHECK
component.description: Each flow Dockerfile has HEALTHCHECK defined. For GPU flows, scripts/healthcheck_training.py checks that the training process is making progress (not stalled).

component.name: MLflow Tracking
component.description: All metrics, params, artifacts logged to DagsHub MLflow. resolve_tracking_uri() reads MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI from .env. No fallback — fails loud if not set.

component.name: Prefect UI
component.description: Prefect server at http://localhost:4200 provides flow/task visibility. Each training run appears as a Prefect flow run with task-level timing.

risk.description: DynUNet OOM on RTX 2070 Super with batch_size=2
risk.mitigation: DynUNet BS=2 tested on RTX 2070 (8 GB) in prior experiments. If OOM, reduce to BS=1 + gradient_accumulation_steps=2.
Fall back to apt-get install r-base in runner stage. Alternatively, run R scripts in a separate container sharing a volume. The Python matplotlib figures (existing) serve as backup.

risk.description: DagsHub MLflow authentication fails during training
risk.mitigation: Verify DAGSHUB_TOKEN and MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI before starting. Pre-flight check in launcher script: mlflow.set_tracking_uri() + list_experiments(). If auth fails, can fall back to local file-based MLflow (mlruns/).

risk.description: DeepVess TIF-to-NIfTI conversion produces wrong orientation/spacing
risk.mitigation: Validate with nibabel after conversion: check shape, affine, dtype. Cross-reference with original TIF metadata. The analysis flow uses sliding_window_inference which is orientation-agnostic, but spacing affects physical-unit metrics (HD95, ASSD).

risk.description: 15 epochs insufficient for meaningful metric separation between conditions
risk.mitigation: 15 epochs is a mechanics test — we care about pipeline correctness, not SOTA numbers. Even small metric differences validate that the statistical pipeline detects them. If all conditions produce identical metrics, the biostatistics will correctly report no significant differences (also a valid result).

risk.description: SWAG subflow fails (second-moment collection requires stable training)
risk.mitigation: SWAG failure for one condition does not block the rest. The "none" post-training method is always available as fallback. If SWAG fails consistently, proceed with method=[none] only (halves Layer B).

risk.description: 2 folds provides low statistical power for bootstrap tests
risk.mitigation: Expected: power analysis will report low achieved power. This is a mechanics test — the power caveat is documented in T6. Full paper_full.yaml uses 3 folds with 50 epochs for adequate power.

risk.description: DeepVess has only 1 labeled ground truth pair (not 7 volumes)
risk.mitigation: Phase 0 Task 0.1 audits the TIF structure. If only 1 GT pair, N=7 comes from splitting the volume into sub-volumes. If splitting is not feasible (too small), proceed with N=1 (per-volume metrics become per-voxel or per-patch statistics instead of per-volume bootstrap).

biostatistician.check: Factorial design is balanced: 1x2x2 = 4 Layer A cells, 2 Layer B, 2 Layer C
ROPE values from reviewer consensus (DSC 0.01, clDice 0.01, HD95 0.5)
Per-volume metrics enable paired bootstrap (N=12 val, N=7 test)
Specification curve captures multiverse (4 conditions x 2 post-training x 2 ensemble)
Variance decomposition: condition vs fold vs residual
Power caveat for 2-fold design documented in T6
15 epochs may not produce enough metric separation — acceptable for mechanics test

ml_platform_engineer.check: STOP protocol: Source=Docker, Tracking=Prefect, Outputs=volume-mounted, Provenance=git+Docker
DagsHub MLflow via MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI (no hardcoded URL)
GPU heartbeat + CUDA guard + structured JSONL logging
R multi-stage Docker build preserves thin Dockerfile pattern

nature_protocols_editor.check: Sample size justification / power analysis (TRIPOD Item 9a)
Platform framing — metrics demonstrate platform capability, not SOTA claims

timeline.step: DeepVess TIF audit + NIfTI conversion + DVC versioning
Create all YAML configs (factorial, experiment, biostatistics, splits)
Docker image rebuild (base-gpu, base-cpu, biostatistics with R, flow images)
Training: 8 runs x 30 min (15 epochs + SWAG subflow)
Analysis: ensemble eval + DeepVess test + per-volume metrics
Biostatistics: DuckDB + stats + R figures + LaTeX tables
Verification: artifact inventory + reproducibility check + test suite
timeline.note: Wall clock estimate includes Docker build time and potential retries. The ~4h budget from the user covers Phases 3-5 (training + analysis + biostats). Phases 0-2 are setup (1.75h) and Phase 6 is verification (0.25h). Total including setup: ~6.6 hours. Training can run overnight (nohup ./scripts/run_local_dynunet_mechanics.sh &).

dependencies.dependency: Docker images must be built before training
Config files must exist before training
DeepVess NIfTI must exist (for analysis flow)
Training runs must be logged to MLflow
Evaluation metrics must be in MLflow

cross_references.ref: Rules 2 (TDD), 17-19 (Docker+Prefect), 22 (single-source config), 27 (debug=production), 29 (zero hardcoded params), 30 (algorithms match literature)
